_meta.purpose: The content-driven selection layer for dashboards. Each entry maps a CONTENT SHAPE / INTENT to a concrete block. To compose: split the content into groups (headline metrics, entity lists, tabular data), match each to the block whose `whenToUse` fits, then order per template.json.
_meta.usage: Pick, do not invent. Fill every placeholder with real content; drop a node only when no content applies. If a group matches no block, STOP and flag for human review.
_meta.patternSource: Each block id resolves in dashboard/blocks.json.

metric-row.intent: Headline KPIs — a few read-only numbers with a trend.
metric-row.whenToUse: 3 equal top-level metrics (value + delta badge + label). Lead the dashboard with these. Read-only, no action, no placeholder icon.

content-grid.intent: A set of ENTITIES, each described by a card — projects, reports, teams, stations.
content-grid.whenToUse: 3–6 rich items where each has a title, status, short description and ONE navigation Link. Use when items deserve prose, not just a table row.
content-grid.note: Compose as a Grid of dashboard.content-card (auto equal-height). No dedicated pattern id yet — build the Grid directly from the item block.

data-table.intent: Tabular records with shared columns — transactions, logs, connections, tickets.
data-table.whenToUse: Many homogeneous rows compared across the SAME columns. Header row + text-cell rows. Read-only unless a real row action exists.

toolbar.whenToUse: Place directly above a data-table ONLY when concrete filters exist. Each Select must carry a real label + value (text:{label,value}). Trailing icon-button actions are optional and need a real glyph. Omit entirely if there is nothing to filter.
